text: Pyramidal and polarized training intensity distributions are more effective than threshold training for middle- and long-distance runners.
Some of the best marathon runners in the world use threshold-dominant training despite evidence favoring pyramidal and polarized approaches.
Training intensity zones can be organized as percentages of goal race pace rather than physiological markers, which allows different periodization types to be compatible within a unified framework.
Training programs should be at least 4 weeks in duration for meaningful assessment of training intensity distribution effects on performance.

Three primary training intensity distribution models exist for distance runners: pyramidal (most volume in low intensity, progressively less at higher intensities), polarized (high volume at low and high intensity, minimal moderate), and threshold (emphasis on moderate/lactate threshold intensity).
